title: The End of Evangelion DVD Video
display_title: The End of Evangelion DVD Video
wikitext:
[]: The End of Evangelion DVD/Video             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Nov 26, 2012 9:07 pm Please forgive me if this question has already been asked countless times before, and it seems like a fairly common mantra in past threads, but can anyone explain why almost all issues of EoE (on DVD or VHS) are ridiculously expensive? The cheapest VHS I could find Amazon was over $25, and that isn't even close to what a brand new copy of the DVD comes to! If anyone remembers when the DVD first came out, how much did it cost, and was it widely available? From what I could gather, EoE was quite rare in circulation, and you can still NGE DVDs at most anime retailers. Proportionally speaking, compared to the NGE boxsets by ADV Films, the EoE is almost ludicrous. The closest equivalent to EoE in Japan would be the Death(true)² + Air/Love is Destructive/I Need You DVD, which comes to around $50.41 USD. I know the DVD release was not the greatest, but at least it's something.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Nov 26, 2012 9:10 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Mon Nov 26, 2012 10:52 pm Yeah, it really sucks considering Manga Entertainments release was god awful compared to the theatrical version with horrible picture quality.

[]: Re: The End of Evangelion DVD/Video             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Nov 29, 2012 12:36 am The MSRP of the Manga Ent. DVD was $29.99 when it was first released in mid-2002. It was fairly easy to find during the time that it was in print. Sadly, that time has long since past. At this point, you have 2 realistic options for getting a legitimate copy: 1) Suck it up and buy an used copy of the US release on eBay. The going rate seems to be about $40, give or take. The video is based off of the original pre-Renewal Japanese DVD set , but with additional filtering that degraded the picture quality significantly. 2) Get the superior Japanese Renewal DVD release. There are 2 variants of it (the standard release and a DTS Collector's Edition ). From there, you can demux the video, mux in the subtitles from the ZX fansub release, and remux it back onto a DVD-R DL disc with no loss in quality. Of course, this takes a lot more technical expertise than just getting the inferior US release, but the increase in visual quality more than makes up for it in my opinion. I would know, since I have a copy of every DVD release of EoE released in both Japan and the US (5 in total). If you do go for the Japanese Renewal release, be advised that it's quite pricey. The standard version goes for $75 including shipping to the US @ Amazon Japan , DTS Collector's Edition goes for $95 at Amazon Japan . You could also buy a copy of either edition from HMV Japan or CDJapan, but their prices aren't any better currently. There's not too much difference between the standard release and the DTS Collector's Edition except for the packaging and audio codec used, so I'd recommend getting the standard release unless you have a high-end home theater audio system.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Nov 29, 2012 3:31 am I'm under the impression that this much cheaper Italian release is based on the Renewal set. You can ignore the Italian dub and sub, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Nov 29, 2012 7:38 pm pwhodges wrote: I'm under the impression that this much cheaper Italian release is based on the Renewal set. You can ignore the Italian dub and sub, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 30, 2012 2:30 am These days Never Twice the Same Color DVDs have played OK on UK sets, even though PAL is the expected encoding, when friends have borrowed Japanese R2 DVDs from me; the digital sets seem to be encoding agnostic. And of course everything plays fine on a PC media player; that and an appropriate HDMI link will drive your big screen, even if it can't handle raw PAL in a DVD player.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Fri Nov 30, 2012 2:48 am Mr. Tines wrote: These days Never Twice the Same Color DVDs have played OK on UK sets, even though PAL is the expected encoding, when friends have borrowed Japanese R2 DVDs from me; the digital sets seem to be encoding agnostic. And of course everything plays fine on a PC media player; that and an appropriate HDMI link will drive your big screen, even if it can't handle raw PAL in a DVD player.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Dec 01, 2012 7:33 pm svenge wrote: Beats having the "PAL experience" of characters sounding like chipmunks and moving like old-timey Charlie Chaplin films...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Dec 01, 2012 7:45 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Except the Italian edition of the "Feature Films", Death True^2+EoE, doesn't suffer of this issue, as I mentioned in the past the video is encoded at 23.976 fps (progressive) with repeat frame flags, pretty much like the Japanese edition except it raises the frame rate to 25 fps rather than 29.97 like in NTSC world, obviously PC players and progressive scan compatible hardware players output the video at the original "film" framerate.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 01, 2012 7:45 pm PAL and NTSC are standards for video signals (that get sent to TVs), the DVDs themselves don't care one way or the other except that the MPEG2 compressed data is tailored in resolution and framerate so that a DVD player can take it and send the correct PAL/NTSC signal to a TV set. ( This is a pretty good link describing it ). When you get screwed up playback it's usually because the DVD player itself is incapable of playing the DVD correctly. Most cheap DVD players aren't very smart and expect only a handful of resolutions and framerates so if a player that is built to output PAL and is given a DVD that is suited to NTSC, it may not be smart enough to play it correctly. HDTV's use the ATSC standard , which is a completely different thing, although most include tuners that decode NTSC/PAL/etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Dec 01, 2012 8:10 pm Ornette wrote: When you get screwed up playback it's usually because the DVD player itself is incapable of playing the DVD correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Dec 01, 2012 9:08 pm I really hope all of this gets standardized someday, but it probably won't happen if the human condition has anything to say about it. I'm perfectly happy with the digital version of End of Evangelion that I found, and even if I wasn't I am definitely not prepared to shell out the clams necessary for a solid copy. I'll wait for the eventual NGE/Movies/NME super-box-set.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Dec 02, 2012 9:36 am riffraff11235 wrote: I really hope all of this gets standardized someday, but it probably won't happen if the human condition has anything to say about it. I'm perfectly happy with the digital version of End of Evangelion that I found, and even if I wasn't I am definitely not prepared to shell out the clams necessary for a solid copy. I'll wait for the eventual NGE/Movies/NME super-box-set.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 26, 2014 1:51 pm Hey, just so I don’t go making a whole new thread out of this one question, I’ll ask it here. What’s the difference between the Japanese Renewal NGE Feature Films disc set and the Feature Films Collector’s disc set with DTS Sound ? Is the only difference really just the audio, or are there other videos and features on the Collector’s set? I’d like to finally get a good quality version of these movies, adding in the English subs myself, and don’t know which I should get and why. (Embarrassingly, I’ve never looked that much into DTS Sound technology.) EDIT: I’m more concerned with whether or not the less compressed DTS sound will eat into the video quality on the DVD. I already have one crappy version of D&R and EoE, and I’d like not to have one video quality less than another, even if by a fraction.

[]: Re: The End of Evangelion DVD/Video             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 26, 2014 9:46 pm Question was already answered in this thread a few posts up. Hope it helps: svenge wrote: 2) Get the superior Japanese Renewal DVD release. There are 2 variants of it (the standard release and a DTS Collector's Edition ). From there, you can demux the video, mux in the subtitles from the ZX fansub release, and remux it back onto a DVD-R DL disc with no loss in quality. Of course, this takes a lot more technical expertise than just getting the inferior US release, but the increase in visual quality more than makes up for it in my opinion. I would know, since I have a copy of every DVD release of EoE released in both Japan and the US (5 in total). If you do go for the Japanese Renewal release, be advised that it's quite pricey. The standard version goes for $75 including shipping to the US @ Amazon Japan , DTS Collector's Edition goes for $95 at Amazon Japan . You could also buy a copy of either edition from HMV Japan or CDJapan, but their prices aren't any better currently. There's not too much difference between the standard release and the DTS Collector's Edition except for the packaging and audio codec used, so I'd recommend getting the standard release unless you have a high-end home theater audio system.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 26, 2014 10:01 pm Oh. Duh. Thanks! Also, prices for these DVDs seems to have lowered since then. The DTS is about $75 while the standard is about $53. I’ll sleep on this information before deciding tomorrow. I’ve bought Blu-ray discs before eventually buying a Blu-ray Player, and I might do the same when it comes to a Home Theater System. We’ll see.
